args.thought:
Plan for Vestas (VWS.CO) true valuation:

1. Recall existing memory/context on VWS.CO from previous sessions
2. Gather current financials: FY2025 results, 2026 guidance, margins, FCF, balance sheet
3. Get market data: current price, market cap, analyst targets, 52-week range
4. Identify key valuation drivers: order intake, backlog, turbine pricing, raw material costs, installation capacity
5. Peer comparison: Siemens Energy, GE Vernova, Nordex - valuation multiples and margins
6. Build valuation scenarios: base/bull/bear with explicit assumptions
7. Apply AI Bubble thesis lens: indirect exposure via energy demand, grid constraints, commodity inflation
8. Present fair value range with confidence level and key risks

Need to check: VWS.CO is Danish listing (CPH exchange) - may require web-fallback routing if FMP premium-only.

results.title: Vestas Annual Report 2025 – Generating value amidst growing ...
highlights:

results.title: Vestas lifts 2026 guidance after record revenue and improved margins in 2025
results.author: Willow Snyder
highlights:
X (Twitter) LinkedIn WhatsApp

Wednesday, March 25 

DONATION





Credit:Vestas

Share  ...    Vestas has raised its financial guidance for 2026 after reporting record revenue and stronger profitability in 2025. The turbine manufacturer delivered revenue of €18.8bn last year, within its €18.5–19.5bn outlook range, while EBIT margin before special items reached 5.7%, at the upper end of its 5–6% guidance, according to annual results published today. Total investments came in at €1.3bn in 2025, slightly above the company’s expected spend of around €1.2bn. Order intake reached 16.3GW across power solutions and service, lifting the combined order backlog to a record €71.9bn. For 2026, Vestas forecasts revenue of €20–22bn, with an EBIT margin before special items of 6–8%. Investments are expected to total about €1.2bn, while the service segment is guided to deliver an EBIT margin before special items of 15.5–17.5%. Group president and chief executive Henrik Andersen said: “Vestas achieved our highest ever revenue and profitability in the upper end of our outlook, representing a solid result in a volatile global business environment.” He added that growth across onshore, offshore and service drove revenue of €18.8bn, with the 5.7% EBIT margin supported by continued improvement in onshore execution and service performance in line with expectations. “The strong performance in Onshore was underlined by absorbing the continued extra costs and investments related to the ramp-up in Offshore and executing our Service recovery plan,” Andersen said. He also pointed to strong market demand, noting that the “growing need for affordable, secure, and sustainable energy” supported order intake of 16.3GW and an all-time high backlog of €71.9bn. Based on the 2025 results and cash flow, Vestas plans to return additional value to shareholders for the second  ...    2. The company achieved revenue of €18.8bn last year against an outlook of €18.5-19.5bn, while its EBIT margin before special items reached 5.7%, in the upper end of the guided 5-6% range, according to annual results published today. highlights:
Why gold is falling now — and why Goldman says buy the dip

Is it time to abandon gold? # Vestas Wind Systems Q4 2025 slides: Record revenue amid service challenges

By

Company News Published 02/05/2026, 06:14 AM

Vestas Wind Systems Q4 2025 slides: Record revenue amid service challenges  ...    Vestas Wind Systems Q4 2025 slides: Record revenue amid service challenges

0NMK1.79%  ...    Vestas Wind Systems reported its full-year and fourth-quarter 2025 results on February 5, 2026, highlighting record annual revenue but facing headwinds in its service business. Despite posting strong overall financial performance for  ...    pressure and service segment challenges. The Danish wind turbine manufacturer achieved all-time high revenue of €18.8 billion for 2025, a 9% increase year-over-year, while improving its EBIT margin to 5.7%. However, Q4 results revealed some challenges, with the EBIT margin before special items declining by 3.1 percentage points compared to the same period last year.  ...    Vestas reported Q4 2025 revenue of €6.27 billion, up 2% from Q4 2024, driven by higher Power Solutions revenue that was  ...    Vestas achieved record financial results for the full year 2025, with gross profit reaching an all-time high of €2.5 billion, up 21% from 2024. The EBIT margin before special items improved to 5.7%, compared to 4.3% in 2024, while Return on Capital Employed (ROCE) increased to 11.8% from 8.0%. The comprehensive income statement shows significant improvements across key metrics: The company maintained a strong balance sheet, ending 2025 with a net cash position of €1.2 billion. Working capital improved to negative €3.1 billion, representing an €830 million improvement compared to Q4 2024. This strong financial  ...    Vestas has also revised its capital structure strategy, aiming to maintain a solid investment grade profile with a net interest-bearing debt to EBITDA ratio between -1x and +1x, while returning at least 40% of net profit to shareholders through dividends and share buybacks. The company has identified several drivers to achieve its long-term financial ambition of a 10% EBIT margin: ## Forward-Looking Statements For 2026, Vestas expects revenue between €20-22 billion and an EBIT margin before special items of 6-8%. The Service segment is projected to generate an EBIT margin of 15.5-17.5%. Total investments for 2026 are expected to be approximately €1.2 billion. The company’s long-term ambitions remain unchanged, with a focus on achieving an EBIT margin of 10%, ROCE of 20%, and free cash flow of 6% of revenue. Vestas also continues to emphasize the value drivers behind wind energy: affordability, security, and sustainability. While Vestas delivered strong overall results for 2025, the market reaction suggests investors may be concerned about the declining Q4 margins and service segment challenges. The company’s ability to execute its service recovery plan and achieve its 2026 guidance will be crucial for regaining investor confidence in the coming quarters.  ...
